ucs.title: [LLM] APT34 (OilRig) DNS-tunnel C2 — Saitama / Helminth / BONDUPDATER beacon pattern
ucs.description: Hunts the long-encoded-subdomain DNS-tunnel C2 channel that is APT34 / Helix Kitten's signature post-exploitation behaviour across the Helminth, ALMA Communicator, ISMAgent, BONDUPDATER, Karkoff and Saitama backdoor families. The actor encodes command/exfil chunks into the leftmost DNS label and beacons to a single attacker-controlled parent zone — high distinct-label cardinality on one (host, parent-domain) tuple is the defining footprint.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Network_Resolution.DNS where DNS.message_type=Query DNS.record_type IN (A,AAAA,TXT,CNAME) by _time DNS.src DNS.query span=10m
| eval labels=split(DNS.query,".")
| eval first_label=mvindex(labels,0)
| eval label_len=len(first_label)
| where label_len>=25 AND match(first_label,"(?i)^[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{25,}$")
| eval parent_domain=mvjoin(mvindex(labels,1,mvcount(labels)-1),".")
| stats count as query_count dc(first_label) as distinct_subdomains avg(label_len) as avg_label_len values(first_label) as sample_labels by DNS.src parent_domain
| where distinct_subdomains>=20 AND avg_label_len>=28
| where NOT match(parent_domain,"(?i)(akamai|cloudfront|azureedge|trafficmanager|amazonaws|googleusercontent|sophosxl|mcafee|symantec|trendmicro|microsoft|windowsupdate|office|akadns)\.")
| sort - distinct_subdomains
ucs.kql:
let _excluded_zones = dynamic(["akadns.net","akamai.net","akamaiedge.net","cloudfront.net","azureedge.net","trafficmanager.net","amazonaws.com","googleusercontent.com","office.com","office365.com","sophosxl.net","mcafee.com","symantec.com","trendmicro.com","windowsupdate.com","microsoft.com"]);
DeviceEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "DnsQueryResponse"
| extend QueryName = tolower(tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).QueryName))
| where isnotempty(QueryName)
| extend Labels = split(QueryName, ".")
| extend FirstLabel = tostring(Labels[0])
| extend LabelLen = strlen(FirstLabel)
| where LabelLen >= 25
| where FirstLabel matches regex @"^[a-z0-9+/=_-]{25,}$"
| extend ParentDomain = strcat_array(array_slice(Labels, 1, -1), ".")
| where not(ParentDomain has_any (_excluded_zones))
| summarize QueryCount = count(),
            DistinctSubdomains = dcount(FirstLabel),
            AvgLabelLen = avg(LabelLen),
            SampleQueries = make_set(QueryName, 5),
            InitiatingProcess = make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName, 5),
            InitiatingProcessCmd = make_set(InitiatingProcessCommandLine, 5),
            FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen = max(Timestamp)
            by DeviceId, DeviceName, ParentDomain
| where DistinctSubdomains >= 20 and AvgLabelLen >= 28
| where QueryCount >= 50
| order by DistinctSubdomains desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _excluded_zones = dynamic(["akadns.net","akamai.net","akamaiedge.net","cloudfront.net","azureedge.net","trafficmanager.net","amazonaws.com","googleusercontent.com","office.com","office365.com","sophosxl.net","mcafee.com","symantec.com","trendmicro.com","windowsupdate.com","microsoft.com"]);
DnsEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where QueryType in ("A","AAAA","TXT","CNAME")
| extend QueryName = tolower(Name)
| where isnotempty(QueryName)
| extend Labels = split(QueryName, ".")
| extend FirstLabel = tostring(Labels[0])
| extend LabelLen = strlen(FirstLabel)
| where LabelLen >= 25
| where FirstLabel matches regex @"^[a-z0-9+/=_-]{25,}$"
| extend ParentDomain = strcat_array(array_slice(Labels, 1, -1), ".")
| where not(ParentDomain has_any (_excluded_zones))
| summarize QueryCount = count(),
            DistinctSubdomains = dcount(FirstLabel),
            AvgLabelLen = avg(LabelLen),
            SampleQueries = make_set(QueryName, 5),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
            by ClientIP, Computer, ParentDomain
| where DistinctSubdomains >= 20 and AvgLabelLen >= 28
| where QueryCount >= 50
| order by DistinctSubdomains desc
ucs.rationale: DNS tunneling with long base32/base64-style left-most labels against an attacker-owned parent zone is the through-line across nearly every APT34 implant Mandiant/MSTIC have documented since 2017 (Helminth → ALMA → ISMAgent → BONDUPDATER → Karkoff → Saitama). Saitama's traffic in particular emits dozens of unique 30+ char subdomains per minute against one 2nd-level zone. Allow-listing the major CDN

ucs.title: [LLM] APT34 TwoFace / RGDoor IIS web shell on Exchange (w3wp.exe spawning recon LOLBINs)
ucs.description: Detects APT34's well-documented Exchange/IIS web-shell tradecraft (TwoFace, RGDoor, HighShell, HyperShell) by catching `w3wp.exe` running an Exchange/OWA/ECP application pool spawning command-line recon, credential, or download utilities — the post-exploit pivot APT34 uses immediately after dropping an ASPX shell on an internet-facing Exchange/SharePoint server (linked to Mandiant's UNC788 / IRN2 cluster work and the 2019 Lab Dookhtegan dump).
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process_cmd values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name=w3wp.exe (Processes.parent_process=*MSExchange* OR Processes.parent_process=*OWA* OR Processes.parent_process=*ECP* OR Processes.parent_process=*RpcProxy* OR Processes.parent_process=*OABFeeder* OR Processes.parent_process=*DefaultAppPool*) Processes.process_name IN (cmd.exe,powershell.exe,pwsh.exe,cscript.exe,wscript.exe,whoami.exe,net.exe,net1.exe,nltest.exe,systeminfo.exe,tasklist.exe,ipconfig.exe,netstat.exe,arp.exe,quser.exe,query.exe,reg.exe,schtasks.exe,wmic.exe,bitsadmin.exe,certutil.exe,curl.exe,ping.exe,hostname.exe) by host Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
| where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("MSExchange", "OWA", "ECP", "RpcProxy", "OABFeeder", "DefaultAppPool", "SharePoint")
| where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe",
                     "whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","nltest.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe",
                     "ipconfig.exe","netstat.exe","arp.exe","quser.exe","query.exe",
                     "reg.exe","schtasks.exe","wmic.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe",
                     "curl.exe","ping.exe","hostname.exe","dsquery.exe","ntdsutil.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain,
          FileName,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          IntegrityLevel = ProcessIntegrityLevel,
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "w3wp.exe"
| where ProcessId has_any ("MSExchange","OWA","ECP","RpcProxy","OABFeeder","DefaultAppPool","SharePoint")
   or CommandLine has_any ("MSExchange","OWA","ECP","DefaultAppPool")
| where NewProcessName has_any ("\\cmd.exe","\\powershell.exe","\\pwsh.exe","\\cscript.exe","\\wscript.exe",
                                "\\whoami.exe","\\net.exe","\\net1.exe","\\nltest.exe","\\systeminfo.exe",
                                "\\tasklist.exe","\\ipconfig.exe","\\netstat.exe","\\arp.exe",
                                "\\quser.exe","\\query.exe","\\reg.exe","\\schtasks.exe",
                                "\\wmic.exe","\\bitsadmin.exe","\\certutil.exe","\\curl.exe",
                                "\\ping.exe","\\hostname.exe","\\dsquery.exe","\\ntdsutil.exe")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, AccountDomain,
          NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT34 TwoFace/RGDoor Web Shell — IIS Worker Spawning Recon LOLBINs
id: 9c2f7d4a-3a5b-4f0a-9e6e-apt34webshell01
status: experimental
description: Detects post-exploitation activity from APT34 (OilRig) ASPX web shells (TwoFace, RGDoor, HighShell) where w3wp.exe in an Exchange/OWA/ECP application pool spawns command-line recon or credential utilities.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0049/
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/twoface-webshell-persistent-access-point-lateral-movement
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/08
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.discovery
  - attack.t1087.002
  - apt34
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  selection_pool:
    ParentCommandLine|contains:
      - 'MSExchange'
      - 'OWA'
      - 'ECP'
      - 'RpcProxy'
      - 'OABFeeder'
      - 'DefaultAppPool'
      - 'SharePoint'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\netstat.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
      - '\schtasks.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\dsquery.exe'
      - '\ntdsutil.exe'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_pool and selection_child
falsepositives:
  - Exchange administrators running diagnostics inside the IIS worker context (rare)
  - Exchange health probes that legitimately invoke cmd/powershell
level: high
ucs.rationale: OilRig's intrusion model since at least 2017 has been: drop a small ASPX/JScript web shell (TwoFace/RGDoor/HighShell) on an internet-facing Exchange or SharePoint server, then pivot via that shell to run AD/host recon and stage credential theft. Because the shell runs in-process under the IIS application pool, every follow-on command is a child of `w3wp.exe`. A correctly-configured Exchange box al
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
